title: Mass. couple sentenced to prison for manslaughter in death of infant foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker sentenced to 3-5 years for the manslaughter of their 10-month-old foster child.
Child died from untreated strep infection, bronchopneumonia, and empyema; conditions could have been treated with timely medical care.
Couple prohibited from future foster caregiving; sentences staggered due to having four other children to care for.

title: Salisbury woman gets life without parole for poisoning boyfriend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Judy Church, 67, received a life sentence without parole for murdering her boyfriend Leroy Fowler with antifreeze.
Church was convicted of first-degree murder after spiking Fowler's Powerade with ethylene glycol in 2022.
Fowler died from poisoning after Church called 911; video showed her taunting him during his suffering.

title: 5 concerning trends from the Red Sox’ ugly start to 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox are off to their worst start since 2019 with a 1-5 record, following a sweep by the Astros.
Boston's pitching struggles manifest in a 5.22 ERA; new pitchers surrendered 11 earned runs over 12 innings.
The lineup's issues include a .208 batting average and 29.4% strikeout rate, contributing to situational hitting woes.

title: Former Patriots CB Stephon Gilmore retires from NFL
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephon Gilmore, five-time Pro Bowl cornerback, announced his NFL retirement on Instagram after 13 seasons.
Gilmore, 35, played for six franchises and won a Super Bowl with the Patriots in 2018.
The 2019 Defensive Player of the Year recorded 617 tackles, 149 deflections, and 32 interceptions in his career.

title: Oracle layoffs begin globally; impact on Mass. workforce unclear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Oracle has started global layoffs, but no cuts are confirmed in Massachusetts according to state filings.
Layoff estimates indicate Oracle could terminate 20,000 to 30,000 jobs, affecting about 18% of its workforce.
Oracle's job cuts align with a September 2025 plan and follow similar reductions in the tech industry.

title: Ex-Patriots captain Kyle Dugger reportedly finds new AFC home
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kyle Dugger signed a one-year deal with the Cincinnati Bengals, his third team in three years.
The former Patriots captain had 42 tackles, two interceptions, and a sack in nine games with the Steelers last season.
Dugger's playing time decreased with the Patriots, leading to a trade to the Steelers for a sixth-round pick.

title: Mass. family flies home from vacation to find truck stolen from Logan Airport garage
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A Boxborough family returned from vacation to find their truck stolen from Logan Airport's central parking garage.
State Police logged the stolen truck into the National Crime Information Center and noted its toll records led to New Jersey.
The vehicle theft is similar to another recent incident at the same garage; both cases remain under investigation.

title: This registered nurse is running Boston for Special Olympics Massachusetts
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Gabby Peduto, a registered nurse from Wakefield, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Special Olympics Massachusetts.
Peduto works with students with complex medical needs and is inspired by their empowerment despite diagnoses.
She aims to raise funds and awareness for Special Olympics Massachusetts, highlighting their focus on athletes' abilities.

title: North Andover police investigate ‘unauthorized’ social media comment about Kelsey Fitzsimmons case
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A former North Andover Police employee allegedly made an unauthorized comment on the department's Facebook about Kelsey Fitzsimmons.
Kelsey Fitzsimmons, ex-police officer, not guilty of assault, plans civil suit against North Andover for last year's shooting incident.
North Andover Police, consulting legal counsel, investigates Facebook breach; plans IT audit to prevent unauthorized social media access.

title: The Federal closes in Waltham after 7 years in business
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: The Federal, a seafood and steak restaurant in Waltham, closed after seven years due to rising costs.
Located at 74 Tower Rd., The Federal struggled despite hopes for nearby development to boost customer traffic.
Sister property Stazione di Federal remains open, offering to honor all outstanding gift cards from The Federal.
